skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies Anthropic-inspired brand colors and typography guidance to visual artifacts, including headings, body text, shapes, and accents.
skill.summary: Apply Anthropic-style colors, fonts, and visual hierarchy to design artifacts.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported network, command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but manual review found these are false positives in Markdown formatting and Apache License text. No executable code, prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution, filesystem access, or network behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Network URL Matches
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are Apache License reference links in LICENSE.txt. They are plain license text and do not instruct the model or runtime to make network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching lines are canonical Apache License URLs. No executable network client, fetch instruction, or data transfer behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts match ordinary prose in the skill description, keyword list, font fallback text, and Apache License terms. No cryptographic API, hashing function, or security-sensitive algorithm appears in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only documentation and license prose at the reported lines. There is no code path that performs cryptography.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Command Matches
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick matches are Markdown inline-code formatting around hex color values. They are visual design tokens, not shell execution syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each reported line contains a Markdown-formatted color value such as a hex code. No command interpreter or dynamic execution mechanism is referenced.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts occur in design documentation and a library reference for RGB color handling. No host inspection, network scanning, discovery command, or data collection behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is color styling and documentation. The file contains no executable script or instruction to perform system or network reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Apply Brand Guidelines to Visual Artifacts
content.value_statement: Teams need consistent visual styling across presentations and design outputs. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concrete color and typography rules for Anthropic-style artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Lists primary light, dark, gray, and accent color values for brand styling.
Defines Poppins for headings and Lora for body text with fallback fonts.
Guides heading, body text, shape, and accent color treatment.
Explains how to preserve readability across different systems.
References RGB color application for presentation-oriented workflows.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automated asset conversion tools.
Requires target artifacts or design context from the user.
Custom fonts must already be available for exact typography results.
Does not provide official legal approval for trademark or brand usage.

use_cases.title: Refresh a presentation deck
use_cases.description: Apply consistent colors, headings, body text, and accent styling to slides before stakeholder review.

use_cases.title: Style internal documentation
use_cases.description: Use the palette and typography guidance to align diagrams, callouts, and document visuals.

use_cases.title: Review design consistency
use_cases.description: Check whether a draft artifact follows the stated color, type, and hierarchy recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Basic Brand Styling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple artifact update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the brand guidelines to this artifact. Use the defined colors, heading font, body font, and accent color rules.

prompt_templates.title: Update a Slide Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this presentation and suggest slide-by-slide styling changes using the brand colors, typography, and shape accent rules.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Visual Style Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise checklist for evaluating whether this artifact follows the brand palette, typography, hierarchy, and readability guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Redesign a Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for applied design direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this draft for brand consistency, identify styling gaps, and provide revised visual direction for text, backgrounds, shapes, and accents.

output_examples.input: A slide uses many unrelated accent colors and default system fonts.
output_examples.output: The result identifies inconsistent colors, recommends the approved accent palette, assigns Poppins to headings, and assigns Lora or Georgia to body text.

output_examples.input: A one-page brief needs a cleaner brand-aligned visual hierarchy.
output_examples.output: The result proposes dark primary text, light backgrounds, restrained gray surfaces, and accent colors only for emphasis.

output_examples.input: A design reviewer wants pass or fail criteria for a draft artifact.
output_examples.output: The result provides a practical checklist covering palette use, font choices, readable contrast, and consistent shape styling.

best_practices: Provide the artifact type, audience, and desired level of visual polish.
Ask for specific recommendations for colors, typography, shapes, and hierarchy.
Verify font availability when exact Poppins and Lora rendering matters.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the guidance as legal approval for trademark use.
Do not apply all accent colors at once when a simpler hierarchy is clearer.
Do not expect the skill to install fonts or run automated conversion scripts.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guide with color, typography, and styling recommendations.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it update presentation styling?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide presentation styling through colors, fonts, text hierarchy, and accent shape rules.

faq.question: Does it install Poppins or Lora fonts?
faq.answer: No. The skill recommends these fonts and fallback fonts, but it does not install anything.

faq.question: Is this an official Anthropic brand approval process?
faq.answer: No. It provides styling guidance only and does not replace legal or brand review.

faq.question: Why were static security findings dismissed?
faq.answer: Manual review found only license URLs, Markdown color formatting, and design prose at the reported locations.
